_doc: Species-specific overrides for the cross-domain syndrome engine. Each entry modifies syndrome interpretation when the study species matches.

rat._notes: Rat is the baseline species — minimal overrides needed.

flags: GLDH/SDH are liver-specific in rats (ALT has muscle/RBC sources). Certainty may improve if these biomarkers are available.
CORT = corticosterone in rats (not cortisol); verify assay specificity.

rabbit._notes: Herbivore with unique physiology: ALT not liver-specific, GGT predominantly renal, cortisol primary glucocorticoid, baseline reticulocytosis 1-4%, N:L ratio ~1:1, calcium excretion via kidneys.

termOverrides.flag: ALT not liver-specific in rabbits (high cardiac muscle activity, ~5h half-life). Verify with SDH or histopathology.
AST elevated by physical restraint in rabbits; may reflect handling stress, not hepatocellular injury.

flags: Check for hepatic lipidosis pattern (BW↓ + FC↓ + liver wt↑) — rabbits develop steatosis from brief anorexia.

termOverrides.flag: GGT predominantly renal in rabbits; reduced sensitivity for hepatobiliary disease. Clotting artifact risk if serum separation delayed.

flags: Consider bile acids (if measured) as more sensitive cholestasis marker than GGT in rabbits.

termOverrides.flag: BUN has high diurnal/dietary variability in rabbits (caecotrophy, protein intake). Creatinine is more reliable anchor.

flags: CA↑ and PHOS↑ are key nephrotoxicity markers in rabbits (renal calcium excretion pathway).
Baseline reticulocytes 1-4% in rabbits — a 2× fold-change may not be meaningful. Adjust regenerative response thresholds.
Heterophils may be misidentified as eosinophils by automated analyzers — verify NEUT reporting in SEND data.
Baseline reticulocytes 1-4% in rabbits — adjust regenerative response thresholds vs other species.
N:L ratio ~1:1 baseline in rabbits; evaluate absolute lymphopenia vs relative shift. Stress causes relative heterophilia + lymphopenia without absolute WBC increase.
CORT in rabbits = cortisol (not corticosterone); verify assay is cortisol-specific.
Adrenaline causes transient lymphocytosis in rabbits (opposite to glucocorticoid lymphopenia); sampling time relative to restraint matters.
Rabbit IKr-driven cardiac repolarization is more translatable to human QT risk than rat Ito-driven repolarization.
ALT elevation may be cardiac-origin in rabbits — cross-check with troponin (cTnI) if available.
Rabbits have lower sensitivity to FCHH enzyme induction pathway than rats. TSH assay validation limited for rabbits.
Hy's Law adapted criteria have reduced specificity in rabbits — ALT is not liver-specific and TBILI elevation may reflect hemolysis or lipidosis.

dog._notes: Gold standard non-rodent species. ALT highly liver-specific (~59h half-life). Unique C-ALP isoenzyme makes ALP least specific in any species. Gold standard for QTc (ICH S7B). SDMA validated for early nephrotoxicity.

termOverrides.flag: ALT is highly liver-specific in dogs (~59h half-life); reliable hepatocellular marker.

flags: Corticosteroids elevate dog ALT ~2-3× — check concurrent ALP/C-ALP elevation for steroid hepatopathy pattern.
C-ALP isoenzyme unique to dogs — ALP elevation without hyperbilirubinemia has high prior probability of reflecting C-ALP, not cholestasis. Verify with bilirubin status.
ALP can reach >100× normal from glucocorticoids alone without true cholestasis.
If ALP↑ + GGT↑ + no BILI↑ → steroid hepatopathy or enzyme induction, not cholestasis.
SDMA (if measured) detects ~20% GFR loss vs creatinine's ~75%; preferred early nephrotoxicity marker in dogs.
Check eosinopenia + neutrophilia + lymphopenia pattern — if all three present, stress leukogram (XS08) is primary, not immunotoxicity.
Spontaneous lymphoma background in aged beagles; verify lymphocytosis is not neoplastic in chronic studies.
CORT = cortisol in dogs. C-ALP elevation is expected downstream of stress cortisol.
Classic stress leukogram: neutrophilia + lymphopenia + monocytosis + eosinopenia (4-marker signature). EOS↓ is uniquely reliable in dogs.
Beagle telemetry is THE gold standard for preclinical QTc assessment per ICH S7B. Maximum translational weight for dog ECG data.
Dogs have lower FCHH sensitivity than rats. Require concurrent liver enzyme induction evidence (hypertrophy, liver weight↑) to confirm pathway.
Hypothyroidism is common in dogs; verify T4↓ is not spontaneous (check HCD).
Keep standard severity. C-ALP may confound R-ratio calculation (ALP denominator inflated).
ALP in dogs requires bilirubin context; isolated ALP↑ insufficient to conclude cholestasis. C-ALP isoenzyme is the most common cause of elevated ALP in dogs.

cynomolgus._notes: Closest to human. Gold standard for biologics. Small n (3-6). Ketamine confound. High background pathology. CD4:CD8 inverted. Human TSH assays don't work.

termOverrides.flag: Ketamine sedation increases AST/ALT in NHPs; verify elevation is consistent with histopathology and dose-response.

flags: High background rate of hepatic mononuclear infiltrates (~33% controls); HCD comparison required for histopath-based evidence.
ALP and GGT significantly higher in male cynomolgus; use sex-stratified references. No C-ALP isoenzyme in NHPs (unlike dogs).
Spontaneous renal lesions common (~26% focal glomerular/infiltrates); HCD comparison required.
Ketamine reduces WBC and lymphocytes; leukocyte changes require cautious interpretation. Protocol-controlled ketamine timing essential.
Normal CD4:CD8 ratio ~0.5 in cynomolgus (inverted vs human ~2:1); do not interpret species-normal ratio as immunosuppression.
For biologics targeting immune cells: expected pharmacological depletion is not adverse immunotoxicity. Verify target biology.
Peripheral blood immunophenotyping (flow cytometry) is far more informative than total lymphocyte count for NHP XS07.
CORT = cortisol in NHPs (like humans). High inter-individual variability from capture/restraint/social stress. Adrenal weight is more reliable stress marker than cortisol.
NHP QTc data carry high translational weight (IKr-driven, comparable to dog/human). Valid alternative to dog for ICH S7B assessment.
FCHH via enzyme induction is far less operative in primates than rats (TBG-bound T4, ~4-6 day half-life).
Human TSH assays do NOT detect NHP TSH — verify species-specific assay was used.
T4↓ + TSH↑ in NHPs more likely reflects direct thyroid toxicity than enzyme induction.
Match HCD by geographic origin (Mauritius vs Vietnamese vs Chinese macaques).

guinea pig._notes: Most physiologically unusual species. ALT reduced specificity. Cannot synthesize vitamin C. IKr-driven QTc (high translation despite being rodent). Complement C4 deficiency in some colonies.

termOverrides.flag: ALT has reduced sensitivity and specificity for liver injury in guinea pigs. Require SDH/GLDH or histopathology for confirmation.

flags: Verify vitamin C supplementation — deficiency elevates liver enzymes and causes anemia independent of test article.
ALT is consistently higher in males than females; apply sex-stratified references.
ALP decreases sharply in first 200 days of life; apply age-stratified reference range. GGT is more informative relative to age-adjusted ALP.
Spontaneous nephrocalcinosis common in guinea pig kidneys; HCD mandatory. CA↑ relevant as supporting nephrotoxicity marker (renal calcium excretion).
Lymphocyte-dominant leukogram (40-80% lymphocytes). Heterophils present — may be misidentified by automated analyzers.
Vitamin C deficiency produces non-regenerative microcytic anemia mimicking myelosuppression — check supplementation before concluding hemolysis.
Complement C4 deficiency exists naturally in some guinea pig colonies; verify complement status for immunotoxicity studies.
Lymphocyte-dominant leukogram (40-80%); LYMPH↓ thresholds differ from dog/NHP baselines.
CORT = corticosterone in guinea pigs (like rats); verify assay specificity.
Anorexia rapidly depletes vitamin C stores (days) — BW↓ + FC↓ may trigger secondary hypovitaminosis C cascade producing multi-organ false toxicity signals.
Guinea pig cardiac repolarization is IKr-driven — exception to rodent QTc rule. CVGP model validated at 92% accuracy for QTc/PR prediction.
If anesthetized CVGP protocol, note urethane anesthesia has own cardiovascular effects.
Limited FCHH data for guinea pigs; default to mechanism_uncertain. TSH assay validation sparse for this species.
ALT not liver-specific in guinea pigs; vitamin C deficiency can produce concurrent ALT+BILI elevation. Require SDH/GLDH and/or histopathology confirmation.
Verify dietary vitamin C adequacy in all guinea pig studies — inadequate supplementation mimics multi-organ systemic toxicity.
Match HCD by breeder/supplier — AST, ALP differ significantly between guinea pig breeders.
